records.ety: [OE. countrepesen, counterpeisen, F. contrepeser. See Counter, adv., and Poise, v. t. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Counterpoised (-poizd`); p. pr. & vb. n. Counterpoising.]

defs: To act against with equal weight; to equal in weight; to balance the weight of; to counterbalance.
To act against with equal power; to balance.
quotes: So many freeholders of English will be able to beard and counterpoise the rest.

records.ety: [OE. countrepese, OF. contrepois, F. contrepods. See Counter, adv., and Poise, n.]

defs: A weight sufficient to balance another, as in the opposite scale of a balance; an equal weight.
quotes: Fastening that to our exact balance, we put a metalline counterpoise into the opposite scale.

defs: An equal power or force acting in opposition; a force sufficient to balance another force.
quotes: The second nobles are a counterpoise to the higher nobility, that they grow not too potent.

defs: The relation of two weights or forces which balance each other; equilibrium; equiponderance.
quotes: The pendulous round eart, with balanced air, In counterpoise.
